mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeds 0.5 percentage points, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity, generating positive short-term equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during inflationary supply shocks, yielding negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when inflation crosses the threshold, contradicting tightening expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movements where nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirm robust labor market health, yielding positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Correlation between proxy signals breaks down during structural labor shifts, rendering the joint signal ineffective.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP revisions erase the initial positive signal within two months, causing forward returns to turn negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth falling below 0.2 percent month-over-month indicates supply-side bottlenecks that constrain economic activity and generate negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn positive despite stagnant industrial production data, invalidating the bottleneck mechanism.
